intro: The clearest split is that Intercom has a free tier while Zendesk does not. Intercom is Customer messaging platform with AI chatbot, in-app chat, push notifications, and product tours for; Zendesk is Enterprise customer service platform with ticketing, live chat, AI, and mobile SDK for in-app. The data points Intercom at mobile app deployment automation and app store metadata management, and Zendesk at mobile app deployment automation and app store metadata management. Pricing: Intercom is subscription from $29/mo; Zendesk is subscription from $19/month. Lock-in is medium for Intercom and high for Zendesk, with DX at 4/5 vs 4/5 and transparency at 3/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, Intercom emphasises mobile app deployment automation and app store metadata management, and Zendesk emphasises mobile app deployment automation and app store metadata management. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits web-only products, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Intercom is for mobile; Zendesk is for mobile; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose Intercom if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is web-only products.
verdict_b: Choose Zendesk if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is web-only products.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $29/mo for Intercom and $19/month for Zendesk. Intercom offers a free tier while Zendesk does not, so initial cost favors Intercom. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Intercom to Zendesk?
faq.a: Intercom is tagged with medium lock-in (messaging data export limited). Zendesk is tagged with high lock-in (support platform lock-in). At least one side is high lock-in, so budget migration time for re-implementing integrations.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Intercom or Zendesk?
faq.a: Both are rated 4/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Zendesk a good alternative to Intercom?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches mobile, since both list the same fit. Zendesk is flagged as not for web-only products, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from Intercom to Zendesk means leaving a free tier behind.
